volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 685 - Section 870)

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: τὸ φάρμακον τοῦτʼ ἄπυρον ἀκτῖνός τʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: to keep this potion hidden in inmost recesses always away from fire and untouched by the sun ’ s warm ray , until I should apply it , newly spread , where I wished . So I had done . And just now , when the moment for action had come , I performed the anointing secretly in the rooms of the house

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: μαλλῷ , σπάσασα κτησίου βοτοῦ λάχνην ,
sections.translation: with a tuft of soft wool which I had plucked from a sheep of our home-flock ; then I folded up my gift , and laid it , unvisited by sunlight , within its hollow chest , as you saw . But as I am going back into the house , I see a thing inexplicable by words and beyond the knowledge of the human mind to understand .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ κάταγμα τυγχάνω ῥίψασά πως
sections.translation: For somehow I happened to have thrown the ball of wool , with which I had been anointing the robe , into the full blaze of the sun ’ s rays . As it grew warm , it ran all into confusion , and quickly crumbled to powder on the ground , like nothing in appearance so much as

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἐκβρώματʼ ἂν βλέψειας ἐν τομῇ ξύλου .
sections.translation: the morsels left by a saw ’ s teeth when wood is cut . It lies just so , fallen . And from the earth , where it lay exposed , clotted foam seethes up , like the rich juice of the blue fruit from the vine of Bacchus when it is poured on the ground .

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: ὥστʼ οὐκ ἔχω τάλαινα ποῖ γνώμης πέσω ·
sections.translation: And so I am distraught , and I do not know to which side my thoughts should fall . I only see that I have brought a terrible deed to completion . For why or in thanks for what should the monster in his death-throes have shown good will to me , because of whom he was dying ? Impossible ! No , he was

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: χρῄζων ἔθελγέ μʼ · ὧν ἐγὼ μεθύστερον ,
sections.translation: enchanting me in order to destroy the man who had shot him . And now too late I gain the knowledge of this , when it can no longer help . Yes , I alone — unless my outlook prove mistaken — I , miserable one , shall completely destroy him ! For I know that the arrow which made the wound harmed

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: Χείρωνα πημήναντα , χὦνπερ ἂν θίγῃ ,
sections.translation: even the god Cheiron , and that it kills all varieties of beasts that it touches . Since it is this same black venom in the blood that has passed out through the wound of Nessus , must it not kill Heracles also ? To my mind , at least , it must . Nevertheless , I am resolved that , if he is to be brought down ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: ταύτῃ σὺν ὁρμῇ κἀμὲ συνθανεῖν ἅμα ·
sections.translation: at the same time I too will die along with him in the selfsame fall . No woman could bear to live with a reputation for evil , if she cares above all that her nature is not evil .

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: ταρβεῖν μὲν ἔργα δείνʼ ἀναγκαίως ἔχει ,
sections.translation: Fear is necessary when terrible deeds are done , but one should not judge one ’ s expectations before the outcome is known .

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν τοῖς μὴ καλοῖς βουλεύμασιν
sections.translation: There is no room for expectation when one ’ s plans are bad , at least the kind of expectation which lends confidence .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀμφὶ τοῖς σφαλεῖσι μὴ ʼξ ἑκουσίας
sections.translation: And yet in the case of those whose mistakes were unwitting , men ’ s anger is softened . So it should be for you .

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: τοιαῦτα δʼ ἂν λέξειεν οὐχ ὁ τοῦ κακοῦ
sections.translation: No , such words are not for one who took part in the wrongdoing ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: κοινωνός , ἀλλʼ ᾧ μηδέν ἐστʼ οἴκοι βαρύ .
sections.translation: but only for him who has no trouble at his own door .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: σιγᾶν ἂν ἁρμόζοι σε τὸν πλείω λόγον ,
sections.translation: It would suit you to refrain from saying anything more , unless you would reveal anything to your own son . For he is here , the one who earlier went to seek his father .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ , ὡς ἂν ἐκ τριῶν σʼ ἓν εἱλόμην ,
sections.translation: O Mother , how I wish I could choose one of three fates for you !

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ἢ μηκέτʼ εἶναι ζῶσαν , ἢ σεσωσμένην
sections.translation: I wish that you no longer lived , or , if you remained , that someone else called you “ Mother ”, or that some new and kinder heart had been exchanged for your present one !

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστίν , ὦ παῖ , πρός γʼ ἐμοῦ στυγούμενον ;
sections.translation: My son , what have I done to cause your hate ?

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρα τὸν σὸν ἴσθι , τὸν δʼ ἐμὸν λέγω
sections.translation: Listen to me ! You have killed

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: πατέρα , κατακτείνασα τῇδʼ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ .
sections.translation: your husband , my father — my father !

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: οἴμοι , τινʼ ἐξήνεγκας , ὦ τέκνον , λόγον ;
sections.translation: oh , no ! What is this you have blurted out , Hyllus ?

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ὃν οὐχ οἷόν τε μὴ οὐ τελεσθῆναι · τὸ γὰρ
sections.translation: Only what cannot but be fulfilled . For who could undo what has happened ?

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας , ὦ παῖ ; τοῦ παρʼ ἀνθρώπων μαθὼν
sections.translation: What do you mean , son ? From whom did you hear this

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἄζηλον οὕτως ἔργον εἰργάσθαι με φής ;
sections.translation: that you charge me with a crime so awful ?

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: αὐτὸς βαρεῖαν ξυμφορὰν ἐν ὄμμασιν
sections.translation: I have seen my father ’ s overwhelming misfortune with my own eyes . I did not learn of it by hearsay .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ἐμπελάζεις τἀνδρὶ καὶ παρίστασαι ;
sections.translation: Where did you find him ? Where were you with him ?

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: εἰ χρὴ μαθεῖν σε , πάντα δὴ φωνεῖν χρεών .
sections.translation: If you need to hear , then I must tell all .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ὅθʼ εἷρπε κλεινὴν Εὐρύτου πέρσας πόλιν ,
sections.translation: After sacking the famous city of Eurytus , he went his way with the trophies and choice spoils of victory . There is a sea-washed headland of Euboea , Cape Cenaeum , where he marked out altars and a sacred grove to the Zeus of his fathers .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: οὗ νιν τὰ πρῶτʼ ἐσεῖδον ἄσμενος πόθῳ .
sections.translation: There I first saw him , to the gratification of my desire . He was about to make a sacrifice rich in offerings when his own herald , Lichas , came to him from home with your gift , the deadly robe , in his hands . This he put on as you prescribed

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ταυροκτονεῖ μὲν δώδεκʼ ἐντελεῖς ἔχων
sections.translation: and then began his offering with twelve bulls , free from blemish , the prime of the spoil ; but altogether he brought a hundred mixed victims to the altar . At first the miserable wretch prayed with serene soul and rejoiced in his ornate garb .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: ὅπως δὲ σεμνῶν ὀργίων ἐδαίετο
sections.translation: But when the blood-fed flame began to blaze from the holy offerings and from the resinous pine , a sweat broke out on his skin and the tunic clung to his sides close-glued at every joint , as if by a craftsman ’ s hand ; there came

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ἀδαγμὸς ἀντίσπαστος · εἶτα φοινίας
sections.translation: a convulsive , biting pain in his bones ; and then the venom , like that of some deadly , cruel viper , began to devour him . At that he shouted for the ill-fated Lichas — who was in no way to blame for your crime — asking by what plots he had brought that robe .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ὁ δʼ οὐδὲν εἰδὼς δύσμορος τὸ σὸν μόνης
sections.translation: But he , unfortunate one , all-unknowing , said that he had brought your gift from you alone , just as it had been sent . When Heracles heard it , just as a piercing spasm clutched his lungs , he caught him by the foot where the ankle bends in the socket

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ῥιπτεῖ πρὸς ἀμφίκλυστον ἐκ πόντου πέτραν ·
sections.translation: and threw him at a surf-beaten rock in the sea , causing the white brain to ooze from his hair , when the crown of his head had been scattered and his blood with it .

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: ἅπας δʼ ἀνηυφήμησεν οἰμωγῇ λεώς ,
sections.translation: But all the people lifted up a cry of astounded grief when they saw that the one was frenzied , and the other destroyed ;

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: κοὐδεὶς ἐτόλμα τἀνδρὸς ἀντίον μολεῖν .
sections.translation: and no one dared to approach the man . For he convulsed down to the ground and up into the air as he shouted and cried out . All around the cliffs resounded , both the steep headlands of Locris and the Euboean capes . But when he was exhausted with repeatedly

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ῥίπτων ἑαυτόν , πολλὰ δʼ οἰμωγῇ βοῶν ,
sections.translation: throwing himself on the ground in his anguish and repeatedly shouting with howls of grief , as he dwelled on his ill-mated marriage with miserable you and his alliance with Oeneus , which , he said , he got for himself as the ruin of his life , then from out of the shrouding altar-smoke

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ὀφθαλμὸν ἄρας εἶδέ μʼ ἐν πολλῷ στρατῷ
sections.translation: he raised his wildly-rolling eyes and saw me weeping among his many troops . He stared at me and called me : “ O my son , come to me . Do not fly from my trouble , not even if you have to share my death . Come , lift me up and away and above all put me

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ὅπου με μή τις ὄψεται βροτῶν ·
sections.translation: in a place where no one can see me . But if you have pity , at least carry me in all speed away from this country so that I may not die here .” When he had laid this command on me , we put him onboard ship and brought him just barely to this land ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: βρυχώμενον σπασμοῖσι · καί νιν αὐτίκα
sections.translation:
while he moaned in his convulsions . And you shall soon see him , either alive or freshly dead .
Such , Mother , are the designs and deeds against my father of which you have been found guilty . May Punishing Justice and the Erinys punish you for them ! Yes , if it be right , that is my prayer .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: θέμις δʼ , ἐπεί μοι τὴν θέμιν σὺ προύβαλες ,
sections.translation: Right it is , for to my eyes you have rejected the right by killing the best and bravest of men in all the world , whose equal you will never see again . Deianeira moves towards the house .

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: τί σῖγʼ ἀφέρπεις ; οὐ κάτοισθʼ ὁθούνεκα
sections.translation: Why do you leave without a word ? Do you not know that your silence pleads your accuser ’ s case ?

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: ἐᾶτʼ ἀφέρπειν · οὖρος ὀφθαλμῶν ἐμῶν
sections.translation: Let her leave . May a fair wind speed her far from my sight ! Why should she falsely keep the dignity of the name “ Mother ,” when she is all unlike a mother in her deeds ? No , let her go — farewell to her . May such delight as

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τὠμῷ δίδωσι πατρί , τήνδʼ αὐτὴ λάβοι .
sections.translation: she gives my father become her own ! Exit Deianeira into the house , followed at a distance by Hyllus .

sections.label: Section 1.821
sections.original: ἴδʼ οἷον , ὦ παῖδες , προσέμιξεν ἄφαρ
sections.translation: See , maidens , how suddenly the divine word of the old prophecy has closed upon us , which allotted this destiny : that when the twelfth year

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.original: δωδέκατος ἄροτος , ἀναδοχὰν τελεῖν πόνων
sections.translation: with its full complement of months should have come to an end , it would complete the undertaking of toils for the true-born son of Zeus ! And that promise rides before a steady wind to sure fulfillment . For how shall he who does not see the light of day

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: ἔτι ποτʼ ἔτʼ ἐπίπονον πόνων ἔχοι θανὼν λατρείαν ;
sections.translation: ever have toilsome servitude any more in death ?

sections.label: Section 1.831
sections.original: εἰ γάρ σφε Κενταύρου φονίᾳ νεφέλᾳ
sections.translation: For if the Centaur ’ s deceitful torture smears his sides with a murderous net , where clings the venom which Death birthed and the gleaming serpent nourished ,

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.original: πῶς ὅδʼ ἂν ἀέλιον ἕτερον ἢ τανῦν ἴδοι ,
sections.translation: how can he look upon tomorrow ’ s sun , when that appalling Hydra-shape grasps him ? Those murderous goads , prepared

sections.label: Section 1.840
sections.original: Νέσσου ὑποφόνια δολιόμυθα κέντρʼ ἐπιζέσαντα .
sections.translation: by the deceptive words of black-haired Nessus , torment him with confused thrashing and seethe on his skin .

sections.label: Section 2.841
sections.original: ὧν ἅδʼ ἁ τλάμων ἄοκνος μεγάλαν προορῶσα
sections.translation: In these matters this miserable lady had no apprehension , when she foresaw in the new marriage a great plague swiftly rushing upon her household . Her own hand applied the drug . But no doubt for the results yielded by a stranger ’ s

sections.label: Section 2.845
sections.original: γνώμας μολόντʼ ὀλεθρίαισι συναλλαγαῖς
sections.translation: counsel , given at a fatal meeting — for these she despairingly groans and sheds a tender dew of thickly-falling tears .

sections.label: Section 2.850
sections.original: ἁ δʼ ἐρχομένα μοῖρα προφαίνει δολίαν
sections.translation: And the coming fate foreshadows a great disaster , contrived by guile .

sections.label: Section 2.852
sections.original: ἔρρωγεν παγὰ δακρύων · κέχυται νόσος , ὦ πόποι ,
sections.translation: Our streaming tears break forth . Ah , no ! An infection pours over him , an illness more to be pitied

sections.label: Section 2.855
sections.original: ἰὼ κελαινὰ λόγχα προμάχου δορός ,
sections.translation: than any suffering that adversaries ever brought upon that glorious hero . Ah , you dark head of the spear foremost in battle , who by your mighty point recently led that swift bride from Oechalia ’ s heights !

sections.label: Section 2.860
sections.original: ἁ δʼ ἀμφίπολος Κύπρις ἄναυδος φανερὰ
sections.translation: But the Cyprian goddess , ministering in silence , has been plainly proved the author of these deeds .

sections.label: Section 863
sections.original: πότερον ἐγὼ μάταιος , ἢ κλύω τινὸς
sections.translation: Is it fancy , or did I just now hear some cry of grief rushing through the house ?

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἠχεῖ τις οὐκ ἄσημον , ἀλλὰ δυστυχῆ
sections.translation: What is this ?

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: κωκυτὸν εἴσω , καί τι καινίζει στέγη .
sections.translation: It is no uncertain sound , but a wail of anguish from inside . The house has some new trouble .

sections.label: Section 868
sections.original: τήνδʼ ὡς κατηφὴς καὶ συνωφρυωμένη
sections.translation: And note how strangely , with what consternation on her brow

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: χωρεῖ πρὸς ἡμᾶς γραῖα σημανοῦσά τι .
sections.translation: that old woman approaches to give us some news .
